source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/01_HAD1717MC(MG)/▶ FDA/1. FDA 510k final 수정사항 반영한 최종 문서/007_Software Validation.pdf

document.title: Software Validation Report for X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG
standards: FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 820
FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 803
CDRH Guidance: General Principles of Software Validation
CDRH Guidance: Off-The-Shelf Software Use in Medical Devices
CDRH Guidance: Guidance for the Submission of Premarket Notifications for Medical Image Management Devices
IEEE 1012-1986, Standard for Software Verification and Validation Plans
IEEE 829-1983, Standard for Software Test Documentation
Guidance for the content of premarket submission for software contained in medical devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software validation report for X-ray Detector HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG covering SRS, SDS, hazards analysis, traceability, test plan, and verification/validation results.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports software validation documentation for FDA/CDRH-referenced medical device software submission and quality system records.
keyPoints: The software level of concern is stated as Moderate (Class B).
The software is described as a middle-ware utility library between the still image detector and the End User Application.
References include FDA Quality System Regulation, CDRH software guidance, IEC 62304, IEC 60601-1, ISO 13485, ISO14971, and IEEE software documentation standards.
Static tests and dynamic tests are reported as approved or passed.
The software version history lists SW version 1.0.0 dated March/15/2019 and firmware version 1.0 dated March/12/2019.

entities.evidence: End User Application (EUA) – upper level software that acquires images from detector using this library and then presents (displays) images to end-user.

concepts.name: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis
concepts.evidence: The model used for the Hazard Evaluation was Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA).

concepts.name: Traceability Analysis
concepts.evidence: The Traceability Table summarizes links (cross-correlation) between enumerated (SRS x.x) or enlisted (SRS-C-xxx) items in SRS, enumerated items in SDS, enumerated potential Hazards (HZ x), respective enlisted static tests and enumerated dynamic tests (TS xxx).

concepts.name: Verification and Validation testing
concepts.evidence: Description of V&V activities at the unit, integration, and system level. System level test protocol, including pass/fail criteria, and tests results.

concepts.name: Waterfall Life Cycle Model
concepts.evidence: Software development process follows classic methodology of Waterfall Life Cycle Model

concepts.name: Bad Pixels Correction
concepts.evidence: Bad Pixels Correction This operation corrects scattered bad (dead) pixels by interpolation with good neighbors.

concepts.name: Line trigger mode
concepts.evidence: When the line mode is set, an image is acquired when a signal is transmitted through the line trigger cable.

concepts.name: AED trigger mode
concepts.evidence: When AED mode is set, an image is obtained by detecting a signal received from x-ray generation.

evidence.claim: The document is a Software Validation Report for X-ray Detector HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Validation For X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG Report No.: A-TCF-5-010 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The report file number is A-TCF-5-010 and revision is Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: File No A-TCF-5-010 Rev. No 0 Date March/15/2019

evidence.claim: The document date is March/15/2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Date March/15/2019

evidence.claim: The report was prepared by R&D team Park, Nam Ho on March/15/2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Park, Nam Ho March/15/2019

evidence.claim: The report was reviewed by R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin on March/16/2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin March/16/2019

evidence.claim: The report was approved by Quality Manager Hur Namkyu on March/16/2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu March/16/2019

evidence.claim: The software level of concern is Moderate (Class B).
evidence.evidenceText: 1. Level of concern: Moderate (Class B)

evidence.claim: The rationale states TFT-detectors use low X-ray irradiation and therefore the level of concern is defined as moderate.
evidence.evidenceText: TFT-detectors use low X-ray irradiation that could not harm patient seriously in any way. Because of that the level of concern is defined as “moderate”.

evidence.claim: The software is an acquisition library for detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: The term “Software” when used from uppercase “S” in respective context of this document assumes this “acquisition library for detectors”.

evidence.claim: The software is middleware between a still image detector and End User Application.
evidence.evidenceText: This is “middle-ware” utility library between still image detector (and its low-level driver) from one side and EUA from the other.

evidence.claim: The software includes setup functions for configuration, Gain/Offset-Calibration, and Bad Pixels Correction.
evidence.evidenceText: It also contains SU to modify software and hardware configuration parameters and preparation of Gain/Offset-Calibration and Bad Pixels’ Correction.

evidence.claim: The software is intended to acquire raw frames from the detector and deliver them to the EUA as images.
evidence.evidenceText: SRS 1 Acquisition Acquire raw frame from detector and deliver it to EUA as image.

evidence.claim: Offset/Gain-Calibration equalizes each pixel transfer function and shall support flat-field correction.
evidence.evidenceText: This operation equalizes transfer functions of each pixel. It shall be at least Flat-Field Correction (FFC) with one flat bright calibration point and one dark frame.

evidence.claim: Bad Pixels Correction corrects scattered bad pixels by interpolation with good neighbors.
evidence.evidenceText: This operation corrects scattered bad (dead) pixels by interpolation with good neighbors.

evidence.claim: Line trigger mode acquires an image when a signal is transmitted through the line trigger cable.
evidence.evidenceText: When the line mode is set, an image is acquired when a signal is transmitted through the line trigger cable.

evidence.claim: AED mode acquires an image by detecting a signal received from X-ray generation.
evidence.evidenceText: When AED mode is set, an image is obtained by detecting a signal received from x-ray generation.

evidence.claim: The development environment includes Windows 10 Pro 64-bit, MS Visual C++ 2019, Ubuntu 14 32-bit, ARM cross gcc compiler, geditor, and Source Insight 4.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Development Environment (SRS-C-DevE): Windows 10 pro 64bit. MS Visual C++ 2019 (MS Visual Studio Professional 2019). Ubuntu 14 32bit Compiler : ARM cross gcc compiler Editor : geditor, Source insight4.0

evidence.claim: The detector operational environment includes Ubuntu 14.0 Linux, at least Intel Core 2, 4 GB RAM, and at least 500 GB hard disk.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector Operational Environment Peripheral hardware : Window10 connected by VMware virtual machine OS : Ubuntu 14.0 Linux CPU/Motherboard : At least Intel Core 2 RAM : 4 GB RAM Hard drive : At least 500 GB hard disk

evidence.claim: The software references FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR Part 820 and Part 803.
evidence.evidenceText: FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 820; FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 803

evidence.claim: The software references CDRH guidance documents on software validation, off-the-shelf software, and premarket notifications for medical image management devices.
evidence.evidenceText: CDRH Guidance: General Principles of Software Validation; CDRH Guidance: Off-The-Shelf Software Use in Medical Devices; CDRH Guidance: Guidance for the Submission of Premarket Notifications for Medical Image Management Devices

evidence.claim: The software references IEC 62304:2006 medical device software life-cycle processes.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62304 [2006] Medical device software - Software life-cycle processes

evidence.claim: The software references ISO14971:2007 risk management for medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: ISO14971 [2007] Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The software development process follows the Waterfall Life Cycle Model.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process follows classic methodology of Waterfall Life Cycle Model

evidence.claim: The hazard evaluation model used was Failure Mode and Effects Analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: The model used for the Hazard Evaluation was Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA).

evidence.claim: Hazard HZ 0001 is mismatched or missing calibration file with low probability and moderate level of concern.
evidence.evidenceText: HZ 0001 Mismatched calibration file (No calibration file) L Moderate Check for the presence of a calibration file in "End User Application".

evidence.claim: Hazard HZ 1001 is stopped data transmission after X-ray exposure because of LAN cable disconnection with low probability and moderate level of concern.
evidence.evidenceText: HZ 1001 Stop data-transmission after X-ray exposure because of disconnection of LAN-cable. L Moderate

evidence.claim: The test plan classifies tests as static and dynamic.
evidence.evidenceText: We classify the test by two groups as the next: Static– expert reviews and analysis of source code and DEBUG release of Software. Dynamic –run Software for test data acquisition and processing

evidence.claim: Static tests and analysis were approved.
evidence.evidenceText: Static Tests and Analysis Topic (subject) of test Result is Ok (approved)

evidence.claim: Dynamic tests TS 0002B, TS 0003B, TS 0004A, TS 0006A, TS 1001A, TS 1002A, and TS 1003A passed.
evidence.evidenceText: TS 0002B Passed; TS 0003B Passed; TS 0004A Passed; TS 0006A Passed; TS 1001A Passed; TS 1002A Passed; TS 1003A Passed

evidence.claim: The report states there were no unsolved bugs subject to safety and effectiveness.
evidence.evidenceText: There were no found unsolved bugs that are subjected to safety and effectiveness in this software.

evidence.claim: Software version 1.0.0 dated March/15/2019 is the initial release for HAD-MD-SW.
evidence.evidenceText: 1.0.0 March/15/2019 HAD-MD-SW Initial release, Dual polynomial fitting calibration Only for proprietary applications.

evidence.claim: Firmware version 1.0 dated March/12/2019 is the initial release for HAD-MD-FW.
evidence.evidenceText: 1.0 March/12/2019 HAD-MD-FW Initial release

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is inferred from source path authoritySignal and cited FDA/CDRH references; the document itself is a manufacturer software validation report, not an FDA-issued document.
OCR/text quality includes typographical or extraction errors such as 'Aril', 'Traceabilty', 'fuction', and inconsistent cross-reference numbering.
Some referenced sections appear internally inconsistent or absent in the extracted text, including references to paragraph 9.2 and SRS 8.2.
Signatures are indicated by column headers, but no handwritten or electronic signature content is present in the extracted text.
